headline: Is International Paper’s Footprint Shift and Governance Refresh Altering The Investment Case For IP?
summary: International Paper recently announced it will permanently close its Carrollton South packaging facility in Carrollton, Texas by the end of the third quarter of 2026, as part of efforts to better align its North American manufacturing footprint with customer demand while providing severance, continued benefits and outplacement support to affected employees. At the same time, International Paper is reshaping its governance and operational profile, adding two seasoned industrial and...

headline: International Paper (IP) To Close Texas Packaging Plant In Five Plant Restructure
summary: International Paper is closing its Carrollton South packaging facility in Texas, affecting 64 employees. The closure is part of a broader restructuring plan that involves five U.S. plants. The move is intended to consolidate the manufacturing footprint and adjust operations. International Paper, traded as NYSE:IP, is taking a step in reshaping its operations with the Carrollton closure. The decision comes as the stock trades around $37.56, with the share price reflecting a mix of shorter...

headline: International Paper (IP) Stock Looks Below Fair Value While Sales Stay Weak
summary: International Paper stock has had a mixed few years for shareholders, with the share price at US$37.56 and a decline over the past year set against a gain of 34.9% over three years, while the valuation checks still suggest the stock leans cheap rather than fully priced in. Recent plant closures, weather related disruptions and earnings uncertainty keep pressure on sentiment, which makes the current valuation all the more important for investors to assess. Over the past three years,...

headline: Is International Paper (IP) Fairly Valued Following Its Texas Facility Closure?
summary: International Paper (IP) is drawing fresh attention after it decided to close its Carrollton South packaging facility in Texas by the end of the third quarter of 2026, as part of an ongoing restructuring of its North American operations. See our latest analysis for International Paper.
